title: Review: monotonic diversifier, UA return type, wallet FVK wiring
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — the P0 bug (wrong FVK derivation) means the core feature this diff ships (real payment addresses instead of stubs) is broken in production. Everything else is secondary.
Confidence: 90% chance of missed bug in the generate_fresh_address → wallet interaction specifically; 20% chance of anything else missed. Uncertainty driven by: (1) cannot see how wallet_restore and wallet_init produce key_bytes vs what the FVK derivation expects — this may have implications beyond what I traced; (2) the full dispatch_payment state machine was not re-read end-to-end. Would raise confidence if: a test existed that calls wallet_init, then try_load_wallet_fvks, then generates an address and verifies it is spendable from the wallet's derived spending key.
